ocr.text: The following data table presents a comprehensive analysis of the impact of various compounds on the P-glycoprotein (P-gp)
transport mechanism, focusing specifically on the regression coefficients, standard errors, and P values associated with each
variable. The analysis examines the effects of different dosages of MEPH, including 2.5 mg/kg, 5 mg/kg, and 20 mg/kg, as well
as a control with nor-MEPH at 5 mg/kg. A notable finding is the regression coefficient of -2.76 for the MEPH 5 mg/kg
treatment, which suggests a significant negative influence on P-gp activity, although the P value of 0.061 indicates that this
result approaches, but does not meet, conventional significance thresholds. Additionally, the standard error of 1.47 provides
insight into the variability of this estimate.
Variable  Regression coefficent  Standard error  P value
Tumor stage  0.84  2.13  0.693
Tumor grade  −1.28  0.90  0.153
Pathological type  0.16  1.19  0.896
Gallstones  0.05  1.18  0.969
Drug treatment
Measure  Testing-onsets (min)  VEH  MEPH 2.5 mg/kg  MEPH 5 mg/kg  MEPH 20 mg/kg  nor-MEPH 5 mg/kg
The measure of testing-onsets, which is recorded at 5 minutes, further contextualizes the timing of these effects. Overall, this
data underlines the complexity of interactions between these compounds and P-gp function, highlighting the need for further
investigation into their pharmacological implications and the potential for different dosing regimens to modulate P-gp activity
effectively.
